Steel Wristband of Strategy  
 

Lore Item
Slot: WRIST
AC: 2
STR: +7 DEX: +7 CHA: -5 INT: +7
Focus: Reagent Conservation III
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL
Class: NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Got a Scroll of Scaled Tactics off of a regular bone dragon in Dragon Necropolis. It is Lore, magic, and no drop. That is the scroll you need for this bracer.

QuoteReply
# Mar 09 2003 at 11:42 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591771973497
15 posts
I picked this up in Kael for guess what.. Nothing... Level 45 Wizard running around with my guildies killing some giants... talk about a sweet sweet deal...

Divintia
45 Wizard on Tribunal Server

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 04 2003 at 10:59 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) hint: OMFG you did? oh wait ya they do drop =)
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 05 2003 at 2:59 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I picked one of these up in the Bazaar last night for 500p!!!!!!
Luclin prices and wizards
QuoteReply
# Dec 09 2002 at 2:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591782300709
8 posts
I think this is a great piece for wizards. INT and DEX are a must, and STR never hurts for carrying that extra loot. As far as the focus we really only have skins and portal frags, but every bit helps. I payed 4500 on luclin for this, i've since seen it as high as 8k. dunno if i payed to much, but i dont really mind. a fine piece by my book.
▲ Page top
item
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2002 at 12:46 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252771750
View User Forum
69 posts
has reagent conservation3 on it for effect now
▲ Page top
Thank you new Bazaar
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2002 at 10:08 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255256871
1 post
I think someone was a bit confused on the pricing system in bazaar, but I just picked this baby up for 100pp on my Necromancer. One hundred platinum my friends, my very own heart attack-inducing deal of legendary proportions. So I'm faced with a dilemma. Would I be better served to sell this at going price (approx. 5,000 but I'm not sure, feel free to correct me) on my server (Luclin) or should I cling to it like my mother's teat because it just so happens to bump me up to 200 int on the nose?

31 Erudite Necro with 400pp if that helps the decision any.
▲ Page top
RE: Thank you new Bazaar
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2002 at 12:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362206917
3 posts
I also got one for way under the going price. I think it was an error on the vendors part. I am hanging on to mine. Great resists and stat booster.
▲ Page top
RE: Thank you new Bazaar
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2002 at 10:38 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591774117885
27 posts
depends on what kind of needs you have... it's a great item for a necromancer... int, neg cha, and saves on bone chips - keep it if you can, sell it if you must

QuoteReply
# Jun 09 2002 at 8:19 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1595780126290
19 posts
The later patch changed the effect on this to Reagent Conservation III
▲ Page top
my thoughts
QuoteReply
# Sep 29 2001 at 8:08 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
just because someone has posted an items value as 20,000pp does not mean that it is worth 20,000pp to everyone else. i wouldn't sell mine unless i was offered 100kpp for it; whereas, say, a rogue would find this item completely worthless and would immediately try to sell it. thats the way VI intended it, so thats the way its going to stay.
▲ Page top
ummmmm
QuoteReply
# Sep 28 2001 at 4:50 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763878907
258 posts
I am a rogue and I was looking for a new bracer and I came across these and thought they were nice. Looked at who can use and how much it is and just about died of laughter. Why would a caster want one of these (compared to other things)?! What caster would pay 20k for one of these?!?! Stupid spooty Verant.
▲ Page top
RE: ummmmm
QuoteReply
# Nov 17 2001 at 4:57 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757687988
View User Forum
111 posts
why would a caster want one. lets see...

1) the int helps with your mana
2) the strength helps you carry stuff
3) the dex helps with your channeling
4) the ac (albeit wimpy) helps you absorb damage.

furthermore, you should be aware the 50+'s casters goal when buying equipment is to stuff as much int as possible into a single slot. this is because the caster would like as many slots as possible free for ac/hp/mana/resists/ect gear. this bracer fufils that desire adequetly (although not terrificly, as the wrist is a popular resist slot for casters and it doesnt give *that* much int to make it worth it to some casters.)

therefore, with that in mind this bracer is just as good for me as it would be for you.
actually i was just thinking how 2 ac is really crappy for me (a mage). if the ac is crappy for a mage, then it must be that much worse for a rogue.
▲ Page top
trade
QuoteReply
# Aug 19 2001 at 8:10 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591766075026
20 posts
i traded a gbs for this.. I saw that my primary slot could be more effectively used,and at level 25, i would not be finding many other wrist slots with 7 int, regardless of the str/dex. i saw it was worth 20k, and he threw in 1100pp. thought it was a good deal
▲ Page top
hmm
QuoteReply
# Jul 15 2001 at 11:53 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Why would ANY caster put this on instead of a blue diamond bracer? When you can do this quest your INT will be maxed anyways. The AC will not do anything...str and dex is ok, but will not make up for the sweet resist you get from th BD Bracer. My friends, THAT is MY 2cp. :)
▲ Page top
RE: hmm
QuoteReply
# Aug 28 2001 at 10:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760131119
View User Forum
330 posts
For one thing, it's not often that I am in a situation that my 90MR with just normal gear isn't good enough. I keep resist jewelry on me just in case I need extra FR or CR or something but it's been a long time since I have not resisted a grav flux in Hate or Kael or something. Saying int is useless is just plain stupid. If high level characters diddn't need int, we'd all have our base int. Sure we have 200+ int but.... we have to GET to 200+ int first. I like high int items like this because it allows me to drop other lower int items for health/ac gear wich I find MUCH more useful for day-to-day use than resist gear. Yes, if I am going to be fighting Faydedar, I load up on fire resist gear. If I am going to Fear, however, I keep my ac/health gear on. It saves my life countless times more than higher resists for spells that are not cast on me and weak AoEs that I resist even without specificly trying to get resists up.

Yes, I drop this for select encounters with uber mobs where the AoEs are particularly nasty. Using resist gear for every day use, however, unless you live in ToV, or something, is just stupid and you're wasting your slot. When you're sitting down and a loathing lich takes a liking to you, it's not resists you want.... it's health. This bracer is amasing. Even more amasing, however, because I am an iksar. I don't know of a higher int bracer that I can wear that isn't a super rare drop off an uber mob. Very few of those, even.
▲ Page top
RE: hmm
QuoteReply
# Sep 29 2001 at 8:11 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250624709
16 posts
amen man

QuoteReply

why not use both the bd bracer and this? duh

RE: hmm
QuoteReply
# Aug 14 2001 at 9:44 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965792734
105 posts
Or why not use two BD bracers? That totals 18 sv all, 22 to cold, and 60 mana... Besides, 20K? Just buy a golden bracer!! That is 5ac 7str 7int... and much much cheaper.
▲ Page top
RE: hmm
QuoteReply
# Sep 21 2001 at 2:56 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591768447649
Scholar
19 posts
Iksar can't wear the Golden Bracer and 7 int is greater than 30 mana when under 200 int. The resistances do nothing for you if you aren't getting cast on or the spells do very little damage or you already resist the spells. I agree that this is a very specific case but you are replying to a post by an Iksar necro so I am just trying to answer your questions from that viewpoint.
▲ Page top
dex...not too bad imho
QuoteReply
# Jul 15 2001 at 5:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248967229
79 posts
first off the int is great for obvious reasons, the str isnt the greatest bonus in the world but it never hurts, and dex is good cause dex is the stuff that helps ya get that message...you regain your concentration(or however its spelled) after youve been smacked for 180 which is always as you all know very good....only thing i dont really like is the ac a lil low for my taste but hey im a necro...what is ac after all. =) well thats my 2 cp hope that clears up some questions a few of ya were askin
▲ Page top
Nice item, Str and Dex bad.
QuoteReply
# Jul 13 2001 at 5:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361336744
20 posts
Hm. This is a pretty nice item. Never a problem with 7 int. But the strength and dex are fairly useless. Of course, they'll drive the price up, even if we're not gonna use them.

Xaenoth Darkwind
Necromancer of Innoruuk

▲ Page top
RE: Nice item, Str and Dex bad.
QuoteReply
# Jul 15 2001 at 8:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965792734
105 posts
Ummm.... str is nice so you can carry more, I assume you are a DE and low level at that since you don't have a prob. with weight yet :), and the dex is good also nice having. Don't ask me why cause I don't know. Just have a few high level necro friend telling me it is.

/shrug

Anyway just wanted to tell you I want the int, but the STR is damn nice having for one who starts out with 60 str.

Vermis
51 necro of Torvonnilous.
